architecture_fit.note: Uses the canonical frozen GPT-2 L5/H3 extraction exactly as the goal specifies and recovers a clean monotonic attn-distance vs edit-distance curve.

baseline_comparison.note: Random-attention baseline (~0.25 flat, ρ=0.13) is plotted alongside the model curve, giving a clear lift of 0.87, though the baseline is framework-provided not attempt-designed.

faithfulness.note: Pure correlational measurement with no ablation/patching; README misdescribes the run as 'hand-built'/'no model execution' when main.py actually runs GPT-2 live.

operating_range.note: Single regime only — edit distance 0-8, seq_len 32, vocab 100; no variation across scale or sequence length to show where it breaks.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: N/A — fixed-model attention extraction, no hand-set circuit despite README's 'hand-built' wording.

visual_judgement.note: Single legible line plot: attn distance vs edit distance with error bars, baseline overlay, and per-bucket n annotations makes the monotonic claim easy to check.

visualisation_rationale.note: Rationale describes a DataFrame and a Spearman-vs-Pearson correlation plot that the app does not actually render, so it does not match the implemented viz.

notes: Numerically strong first pass: GPT-2 L5/H3 attention distance increases monotonically with Levenshtein edit distance (Spearman ρ=1.0, 0.87 lift over a random-attention baseline). But it is a thin, purely correlational result with no causal/ablation evidence and only a single input regime, and the README materially misdescribes the method as a 'hand-built, no-execution' attempt when main.py runs GPT-2 live on GPU. Mean of scored items ~3.0 → borderline.
